EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure system over the Atlantic generated moderate to large long period northwest swells that combined with breezy east to northeast winds which affected the coastal waters of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Breaking waves and resultant high surf caused flooding of the Vega Baja public beach parking lot and partial flooding of the coastal road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure system over the Atlantic generated moderate to large long period northwest swells that combined with breezy east to northeast winds which affected the coastal waters of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Breaking waves and resultant high surf caused flooding of Marina Avenue and forced the closing of nearby schools San Carlos and the Eladio G. Vega in the municipality of Aguadilla. Fisherman boats were pushed to shore.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low pressure system over the Atlantic generated moderate to large long period northwest swells that combined with breezy east to northeast winds which affected the coastal waters of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Breaking waves and resultant high surf caused flooding of the Loiza Headstart in the Colobo sector...road 187 in Vacia Talega sector and streets of the Parcelas Suarez sector.
Heavy showers associated to a strong cold front produced rainfall accumulations of two to six inches. These showers flooded parts of Encantada Community and a road at Barrio Matienzo.
Road 31 at exit 22 near River Rio Blanco and Highway 53 flooded.
Barrio Canas was reported flooded.
Ten apartments were reported flooded at the Monte Hatillo Housing Complex.

Coastal areas of the Aguadilla municipality were flooded by 12 to 15 feet swells that flooded roads and forced several schools to close. Several fishermen boats were damaged as well as boat ramps and platforms at Barrio Higuey. Forty-five feet of the boardwalk were damaged by the heavy seas and debris was accumulated along Yumet Avenue.
A 17 year old surfer drowned as he was swept by rip currents at Barrio  Islote in Arecibo.
Emergency management officials indicated that a part of River Cibuco was out of its banks and flooding low lying sections of roads 620, 647 and 675 which were impassable.
A rockslide occurred on Highway 131 and was partially blocking the highway in the municipality of Quebradillas.
Flooding was reported at Barriada Figueroa in Santurce. Some flooding of urban areas was reported in Condado and Ocean Park. Fernandez Juncos Avenue was closed with 2 feet of water.
Coastal flooding was reported along low lying areas of Highway 102 in Mayaguez. High swells pushed debris into the road in an area were the water breaker wall was damaged.
Road 30 at La Pitina sector was reported impassable.
River Manati was reported out of its banks and flooding road 666 at the Cachete sector.
Landslide reported along road 717 at Barrio Palomas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Broad area of low pressure in the mid to upper levels located across the Atlantic and extending south across Hispaniola and Puerto Rico combined with local conditions to produce heavy rain in the San Juan metro and vicinity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A flooded home was reported at Villa Conquistador, Barrio San Isidro.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary frontal trough that extended from just south of the Dominican Republic northeast across the Mona Passage  into the Central Atlantic and strong upper level jet combined to generate numerous showers and isolated thunderstorms across the northern section of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported along Baldorioty Avenue near public housing, right two lanes were flooded from Carolina to San Juan.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Broad area of low pressure in the mid to upper levels located across the Atlantic and extending south across Hispaniola and Puerto Rico combined with local conditions to produce heavy rain in the San Juan metro and vicinity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding and trapped vehicles were reported along 65th Infanteria avenue in Rio Piedras.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: One home was reported flooded at Urb. Parque del Monte. One car and its occupant were trapped by flood waters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Frontal trough that extended from just south of Dominican Republic northeast across the Mona Passage and then continued northeast into the central Atlantic was responsible for the moderate to heavy rains that affected parts of Puerto Rico from mid afternoon Friday March 31 through the early morning of Saturday April 1.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding forced several families to evacuate their homes in Barrio Borinquen and Camaseyes of Aguadilla. At least 10 families lost all their personal belongings and other 90 suffered partial losses. Roads 459, 467, and 468 reported damage as well as Marina Street and Parterre.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nearby mid to upper trough combined with daytime heating and local effects to produce numerous showers and isolated thunderstorms. Flooding was reported across Manati and Barceloneta during the afternoon. Doppler weather radar estimated up to five inches of rainfall there.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A section of the old road to Barceloneta was flooded and a car was trapped in the flood waters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: NWS doppler radar indicated a line of strong showers and thunderstorms producing heavy rain over the area of Quebradillas and Isabela. Fifteen homes in Quebradillas and six in Isabela were flooded as a result of the rains generated by an mid to upper level trough across the area in combination with an old frontal boundary over the local Atlantic waters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road from the municipality of Quebradillas to Isabela was reported flooded

A 30 year old man died after being struck by lightning at Barrio Islote in the Arecibo municipality.

River Culebrinas at Barrio Calabaza was reported flooding to the bridge at route 119. The intersection of Highway 111 and 441 was reported flooded at Barrio Guatemala. Street flooding reported in Los Alamos neighborhood. Santa Teresita neighborhood in Juncal was flooded.

AT 903 pm the  U.S.G.S. lower Culebrinas river level sensor indicated that the water levels were above flood stage and still increasing. News media reported following morning that road 115 along Barrio Espinal and Tablonal of Aguada was flooded during the night.
Road134 was reported flooded in Barrio Bayaney.
Street flooding was reported in Paseo del Prado neighborhood as rainfall runoff flooded three homes.

Flooded homes and streets were reported in Barrio Guerrero, along road 459 Altura del Poblado San Antonio, road 115, Highway 2 in front of Walgreens, Rafael Hernandez street and Victoria Urbanization.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast wind flow prevailed across Puerto Rico on Sunday and combined with local diurnal effects to produce scattered showers and thunderstorms mainly during the afternoon. The heaviest rain occurred over the northeast section between Naguabo...Ceiba and Fajardo municipalities where the Doppler radar rainfall estimates indicated over four inches of rain in localized areas. This caused a rapid rise of River Fajardo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 973 at El Duke sector in Naguabo was reported flooded and impassable. Highway 3 from Naguabo towards Daguo was reported flooded and impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave over Hispaniola produced an area of rain and showers that extended across eastern Puerto Rico during the evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 31 at La Pitina sector in Naguabo was reported flooded and impassable
Urban and street flooding was reported along Bechara sector in Puerto Nuevo and in Las Americas neighborhood in Rio Piedras
Urban and street flooding was reported in Barrio Cacao and  Parcelas Grandes.
Small stream was reported flooding highway 3 in Yabucoa.
River Maunabo level was reported high but within its banks.
Urban and street flooding was reported along Progreso, Betances, Munoz Rivera, Llano Jimenez streets.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban and street flooding was reported along Union and Marcelo Gotay streets.
Small stream Machuchal in the Vega ALta muncipality was reported out of its banks.
Off duty National Weather Service employees reported urban flooding in San Juan near the entrance to the Minillas Tunnel and in some sections of Levittown.
Downed trees and power lines were reported in the Cabo Rojo municipality as thunderstorms moved through the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall developed over the northwestern section of Puerto Rico during the late afternoon. Doppler radar estimated rainfall totals varied from two to three inches over this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Quebrada Larga de Anasco small stream was reported out of its banks at Barrio Las Marias.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical wave was moving west across Puerto Rico generating scattered showers and isolated thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban and street flooding reported along road 834.

Several sectors of the San Sebastian municipality were reported flooded, Barrio Juncal, Guatemala, Pueblo Nuevo, and several downtown streets.  River Guatemala was reported out of its banks at Barrio Guatemala flooding the Fey Mendez Park and some nearby businesses. Another stream was reported out of its banks at Comunidad Gonzalez near the 4H park.
A landslide was reported along road 495 in Barrio Perchas 2 of San Sebastian.

Mayaguez City emergency managers reported a tornado touched down at the SAM's parking lot at the Western Plaza in Mayaguez. The funnel cloud originated inland near Cerro Las Mesas and traveled west towards Barrio El Mani. One tree was uprooted and one automobile was damaged. The winds tossed several shopping carts around.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous showers and thunderstorms trailing behind a tropical wave across the Mona Passage generated heavy rains across the southern municipalities of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 2 along Balneario El Tuque, and road in front of Mercedita airport, were reported flooded.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 2 in Penuelas was reported flooded.
Urban flooding was reported.
Urban flooding was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong showers and thunderstorms developed across the west and southwest section of Puerto Rico during the early afternoon in response to an upper level trough that was approaching from the east. The interaction of the trough with local effects enhanced shower and thundersorm development.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Street and road flooding was reported in the municipality of Hormigueros. Three homes were flooded at Barrio Jaguita, Lavadero and Estancias del Rio.  Roads Guamani, 104, 3, 346, 114 were reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and isolated thunderstorms...some with very heavy rain affected the San Juan metro and adjacent areas. Periods of heavy rainfall and some urban flooding were reported in a few areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers and isolated thunderstorms occurred over parts of the San Juan metropolitan area causing periods of heavy rainfall and some urban and street flooding in the Catano municipality. . Amparo, Nueva and Gomez streets were flooded in Barriada Juana Matos. The wetland along road 5 in front of Puente Blanco neighborhood was close to overflowing its banks. Atalaya and Reparto streets were reported flooded.
Road 31 was reported flooded and impassable at Sector La Fe in the Naguabo municipality.
Road 849 at Barrio Santo Domingo in Rio Piedras was reported flooded at km 1.7.
Road flooding was reported near Villa Capri in Trujillo Alto, just before the new bridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated strong thunderstorms affected the San Sebastian...Adjuntas and Lares sectors producing gusty winds and heavy rains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed trees were reported along road 111 towards Lares,  Barrio Los Angeles. A tree fell on top of an automobile.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low to  mid level trough moved across Puerto Rico and scattered showers and isolated thunderstorms with heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Street and home flooding was reported at Barriada Estevez in Aguadilla.
A flooded road was reported at Montebru sector in front of Walgreens.
Urban flooding was reported at Vista Verde neighborhood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A band of showers and thunderstorms, the leading edge of a tropical wave, moved over Puerto Rico producing strong wind gusts and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree fell on top of car injuring one person.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban and street flooding reported at Segovia street in Vistamar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active tropical wave moved across Puerto Rico and produced strong gusty winds as well as heavy showers and squalls. The morning weather balloon wind report and the Doppler wind profile measured significant winds. A wind advisory was in effect for sustained winds of 25 to 35 mph and gusts reaching 40 to 45 mph. Downed power lines, poles and trees were reported across the eastern half of the Island during the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed power lines and poles at Villa Margarita.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers developed since mid morning mainly across the eastern half of Puerto Rico. A few thunderstorms were also reported near the San Juan metro area. Urban and street flooding was reported in some areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported along road 834 in Guaynabo municipality.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers developed since mid morning mainly across the eastern half of Puerto Rico. A few thunderstorms were also reported near the San Juan metro area. Urban and street flooding was reported in some areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road flooding was reported along Highway 1 at the Flamingo Motel entrance in Caguas municipality.
Urban and street flooding was reported at Parcelas Loarte, Estancias de Florida and Barrio Catanito.
A sign blew over at Walmart Store on Highway 2 and fell on top of a car.   Pea size hail was reported in Manati municipality.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active tropical wave moved across Puerto Rico and produced strong gusty winds as well as heavy showers and squalls. The morning weather balloon wind report and the Doppler wind profile measured significant winds. A wind advisory was in effect for sustained winds of 25 to 35 mph and gusts reaching 40 to 45 mph. Downed power lines, poles and trees were reported across the eastern half of the Island during the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed power lines in Sanat Juanita.

River Cilitos level was rising steadily at the traffic light on Highway 3. River Fajardo went out of its banks  flooding Highway 53 and Santa Rita road. Streets in Maternillo and Mansion del Sapo were impassable. Highway 3 from Fajardo to Rio Grande was closed to traffic due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical wave moved across Puerto Rico and interacted with upper trough across Hispaniola to produce scattered to numerous showers and locally heavy rainfall accumulations. By 11 A.M. the U.S. Geological Survey sensors reported significant rainfall accumulations over the interior section of Puerto Rico. Rio de Bayamon at Arenas..5.98 inches.  Rio de Bayamon below Lago de Cidra ..5.21 inches. Rio de La Plata at Comerio ..4.46 inches. Vaqueria EL Mimo Caguas 3.72 inches. Bairoa Arriba at Aguas Buenas 3.88 inches. Around one hundred families in the municipalities of Caguas and Aguas Buenas were affected by flash floods produced by this tropical wave. Fifty two families in the Aguas Buenas municipality experienced moderate to major flood damage to their homes. Barrio Mulas, Sumidero, Caguitas and Jaguitas were the most affected. In Caguas municipality, 32 homes in El Fanguito sector were flooded, 20 in Villa Nueva and 12 in Villa del Rey. Thirteen sectors in Caguas suffered landslides and seventeen areas were flooded.  The following rivers experienced flooding or reached flood stage...River Fajardo... Blanco... Guayanes...Caguitas...Gurabo...Grande de Loiza and Bayamon.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wood home was struck by lightning at Playa Punta Figuera sector in Ceiba.
A home at barriada Juan Sanchez was reported flooded
A home was reported flooded at Barrio Guaraguao.
A home was reported at barrio Campo Bello due to small stream out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active tropical wave moved across Puerto Rico and produced strong gusty winds as well as heavy showers and squalls. The morning weather balloon wind report and the Doppler wind profile measured significant winds. A wind advisory was in effect for sustained winds of 25 to 35 mph and gusts reaching 40 to 45 mph. Downed power lines, poles and trees were reported across the eastern half of the Island during the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed tree in front of Banco Santander.
Urban flooding reported in San Jose, flooded home at Urb. Rexach, and the Bechara sector was flooded.
Roads 109, 115 and 402 were reported flooded in the municipality of Anasco.
USGS river level sensor indicated that the water levels along river Rio Grande de Arecibo in Adjuntas at Highway 135 and the river Rio Pellejas were above flood stage and rising.
Two flooded homes were reported at Alturas de Parque Ecuestre, and Estancias del Parque.
Landslide was reported along road 123 in Sector Salto Arriba of Utuado.
Small stream at Barrio Guarico was out of its banks.
Spotter reported 2.25 inches during last hour and river Culebrinas level rapidly rising.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical wave moved through Puerto Rico and generated scattered to numerous and isolated thunderstorms...which produced intense rains and moderate to severe urban flooding over the San Juan Metro area. An NWS spotter reported rainfall accumulations of around six inches in Rio Piedras.  Flash flooding was reported in the San Juan Metro area and 150 students were evacuated from a school in Hato Rey. In the Parque Central neighborhood in Hato Rey the flood water level was reported reaching four feet. Homes and businesses were flooded in the San Jose sector near the Barbosa Avenue. Around 15 homes were flooded in El Vedado neighborhood in Hato Rey. Flooding was reported in Puerto Nuevo streets.  Road PR-5 was reported impassable in Cataño City. USGS sensors reported rainfall for the 24 hour period ending at 8 am on August 16 indicated 7.02 inches at Lago Las Curias in Cupey, and 6.68 inches in Hato Rey.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported in San Jose and along 65th Infantry avenue.
Landslide was reported at Haciendas de Carraizo along road 175.
Landslide was reported at Barrio Carraizo after the dam.
A landslide was reported at Barrio La Gloria, Km 12.9, road 941, in front of Francis Mechanic.
Urban flooding was reported along 65 Infantry Avenue, Road 181, from Carolina to Rio Piedras. A car and its passenger were stranded.
Urban flooding was reported along Barbosa street, at De Diego intersection, a car was reported stranded.
Flooding of homes was reported at El Vedado in Hato Rey. Urban flooding was reported in Villa Caparra.
Urban flooding reported along Kennedy Avenue.
Spotter reported urban flooding in Colombus Residential area and several other streets of downtown Mayaguez.
Spotter reported River Cuebrinas flooding roads 418 and 115 in Aguada.
A section of Highway 3 was reported flooded and impassable due to River Fajardo overflowing its banks.
Heavy rain was reported over Culebra in association with rain bands associated with tropical storm Chris.
Small stream Machuchal was reported out of its banks.
USGS river level sensors along Rio Piedras at Cupey, El Señorial, and Caimito were above flood stage and rising.
Localized flooding was reported in Rio Piedras.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface low pressure system that lingered around to the Northwest of the U.S. Virgin Islands combined with a mid to upper level trough to produce heavy rain and flooding. The heaviest rainfall was focused over the San Lorenzo area (headwaters of the Guayanes and Loiza basins) East-Southeast Puerto Rico where local, small stream and urban flooding was in the moderate category.  The more impressive and widespread flooding across the Forecast Area occurred from the 18th to the 20th of October.  The rainfall was of the ���pulsing��� variety with some areas experiencing periods of torrential downpours starting late on October 18th and ending of October 20th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: River Cibuco was reported rising and flooding road 620 in sector Fatima.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated showers and thunderstorms developed over interior Puerto Rico during the afternoon and diminished quickly after sunset. The heaviest rainfall fell over sections of Lares and Utuado municipalities, where over four inches of rain fell in isolated locations, causing River Guajataca in Lares to crest above flood stage early in the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: USGS river level sensor at Lares indicated that the water levels along the Guajataca River in Lares were above flood stage of 13 feet and rising.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level dynamics combined with abundant low level moisture caused significant shower activity across Puerto Rico during the evening of November 13. There was some flooding in the area stretching from the southeast through the northeast portion of Puerto Rico around mid-November where embedded heavy rain resulted in amounts that ranged from 3.29 inches in Naguabo to 5.12 inches in Naranjito.  Significant rises were noted on River Guayanes along the southeast and River Guajataca along the northwest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: USGS river level sensor for Guajataca river at Lares rose above flood stage of 13 feet and crested at 14.13 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak disturbance in the low level wind flow passed south of Puerto Rico and interacted with an approaching upper trough and an old frontal boundary. Showers and a few thunderstorms developed during the evening and caused heavy rains over the municipalities of Ciales, Arecibo, Manati, Florida, Vega Alta, Toa Alta, Dorado and Toa Baja.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains and gusty winds were reported in La Playita sector of Morovis municipality during the late afternoon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers and isolated thunderstorms with very heavy rain developed during the afternoon across the northwest section of Puerto Rico, mainly from the municipalities of Anasco to San Sebastian and Isabela and from Aguada to Aguadilla.  The heaviest rains occurred in San Sebastian and Moca. By mid to late afternoon moderate showers and isolated thunderstorms were still affecting the municipality of San Sebastian. An NWS spotter from San Sebastian reported around 4 inches from 1430-1700 AST. The USGS river level sensor at Culebrinas in Moca indicated that the water levels were above flood stage during the early evening, while the USGS sensor at the Margarita Dam indicated the water level was gradually increasing. According to NWS spotter at Coloso Valley the river level rose along the flood control channels, flooding around 400 acres in the lower Coloso Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers and isolated thunderstorms developed across the west and northwest section of Puerto Rico during the afternoon and produced periods of heavy rain that fed into the Culebrinas river basin. From 2030 LST through 0015 LST on December 7 the USGS river level sensor at Margarita Dam in Aguadilla indicated water levels at 14.97 feet. Flood stage is 14 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak disturbance in the low level wind flow passed south of Puerto Rico and interacted with an approaching upper trough and an old frontal boundary. Showers and a few thunderstorms developed during the evening and caused heavy rains over the municipalities of Ciales, Arecibo, Manati, Florida, Vega Alta, Toa Alta, Dorado and Toa Baja.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 2 was reported closed to trafic between Vega Alta and Vega Baja municipalities. Several small streams were reported overflowing their  banks in Vega Alta municipality.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough across Hispaniola and the Mona Passage and a tropical wave that was approaching the Lesser Antilles combined to maintain moist and unstable conditions across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several residents of the San Juan Metro area reported flooded roads.
